meaning: adj.好看的,漂亮的,出色的,利落的,绝妙的
examples: He is looking pretty spiffy in a white tux and black bow-tie.
Customers are offered preapproved loans for a spiffy new vehicle.
You look spiffy in your new outfit.
Horsell, 39, got a spiffy residence he could then sell for more than it would have been worth unadorned.
Planned systems and market based systems are pretty much the same in invention: the creation of spiffy new things.
